_readme: Cataloged metrics that are deliberately NOT on a panel, each with the reason
somebody chose to write. build_dashboard.py fails on an unpanelled metric that is
not listed here, on a listed metric the catalog no longer has (a waiver that
outlives its metric is a comment pretending to be a decision), and on an entry
The bar for an entry is 'a panel would be actively misleading or structurally
impossible', not 'I did not get to it'. A metric worth emitting is almost always
worth a panel: the builder derives the query, the aggregation and the title from
spec/signal-catalog.json, so adding one is a single line in a boards/*.py
SECTIONS list. That is why this list is currently empty - not because the gate is
toothless, but because the cheap thing to do is the correct thing to do.
Two classes that look like they belong here and do NOT:
* Histogram base names. They are only ever queried through their _bucket/_sum/
_count series, and catalog.metrics_referenced_by() matches those directly,
so a histogram on a panel is credited without a waiver.
* Opt-in or beta collectors (blob ingest, message trace, MDCA, GSA). An empty
panel that names its opt-in is useful; a missing panel is not.
